description: A salmon fillet or steak cooked over direct heat until lightly charred outside and tender inside. It is commonly seasoned simply with salt, pepper, lemon, herbs, and oil so the flavor of the fish remains central.
preparation_steps: Pat the salmon dry and season it.
Lightly oil the fish or grill surface.
Grill until the flesh is cooked through but still moist.
Finish with lemon, butter, herbs, or sauce if desired.

nutrition.carbohydrates: Low unless served with a sweet glaze
nutrition.fat: Moderate to high, largely from the fish itself

health_advice.benefits: Salmon is rich in protein and commonly valued for omega-3 fats.
health_advice.risks: Overcooking can dry the fish, and heavy sugary glazes or large amounts of butter can increase calories.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Use simple seasoning and pair with vegetables or a whole-grain side.

dietary_notes.note: Usually gluten-free when prepared without soy sauce, flour-based seasonings, or breaded coatings.

allergens: Possible dairy if butter sauce is used
Possible soy or gluten in some marinades
